event: Oxygen saturation improved to 100% with non-rebreather mask at 15 L/min
Point of care ultrasound demonstrated McConnell's sign
CTPA demonstrated pulmonary emboli involving bilateral pulmonary arteries
CTPA demonstrated enlargement of pulmonary trunk
CTPA demonstrated flattening of interventricular septum
Right ventricle to left ventricle ratio of 1.4 to 1
Started intravenous unfractionated heparin infusion (goal PTT 60–80 s)
Persistently hypotensive with systolic blood pressure <90 mmHg
Underwent catheter-directed thrombolysis of bilateral pulmonary arteries
Oxygen saturation of 65% requiring non-rebreather mask
Return of spontaneous circulation after CPR and electrical shock
Vasopressor support initiated (norepinephrine, epinephrine, vasopressin, phenylephrine)
Pulmonary angiogram demonstrated worsening bilateral pulmonary emboli
Repeat catheter-directed thrombolysis of bilateral pulmonary arteries performed
Continuous systemic IV heparin infusion continued
Repeat pulmonary angiography demonstrated improvement in right central pulmonary embolism
Venous duplex ultrasound demonstrated non-occlusive thrombus in right femoral vein
Venous duplex ultrasound demonstrated non-occlusive thrombus in right popliteal vein
Anticoagulation regimen transitioned from UFH to LMWH (Enoxaparin, 1 mg/kg every 12 h)
Transitioned to rivaroxaban 15 mg twice daily
Discharged to an acute rehabilitation facility
